# AI Rules And Skills Sync

This project's IDE rule files and skill folders are generated copies.

## Source Of Truth

Do not hand-edit the generated AI rule or skill files inside `CareNet 2`.

Edit the shared source pack here instead:

`C:\Users\callz\OneDrive\Documents\My Projects\SynologyDrive\Websites\Rules&Skills\packs\carenet-2`

## After Editing The Pack

From the `CareNet 2` project root, run:

```powershell
npm run ai:sync
```

## What Gets Regenerated By `ai:sync`

- `.cursor/rules/`
- `.cursor/skills/`
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- `.rules`
- `rules.md`
- `.windsurfrules`
- `.kilocode/rules/tech-stack.md`
- `.kilocode/rules.md`
- `.kilocode/skills/`
- `.agents/rules/tech-stack.md`
- `.agents/skills/`
- `.agent/rules/tech-stack.md`

## What Is NOT Regenerated (Copilot-Specific — Edit Directly)

These files live in `.github/` and are managed directly in this repo.
They are **not** overwritten by `npm run ai:sync`:

```
.github/
  agents/
    feature-builder.agent.md     ← Main coding agent
    code-reviewer.agent.md        ← Read-only review agent
    test-runner.agent.md          ← Vitest + Playwright agent
    migration-author.agent.md     ← Supabase migration agent
    feature-planner.agent.md      ← Spec-writing / planning agent
  prompts/
    new-feature.prompt.md         ← Start a new feature
    code-review.prompt.md         ← Review code
    new-migration.prompt.md       ← Write a DB migration
    write-tests.prompt.md         ← Write tests
    fix-bug.prompt.md             ← Diagnose and fix bugs
    refactor.prompt.md            ← Refactor without behavior change
  skills/
    fullstack-carenet/SKILL.md    ← General feature skill
    supabase-dexie-sync/SKILL.md  ← Data layer skill
    tailwind-radix-motion/SKILL.md← UI skill
    i18next-bangla/SKILL.md       ← Localization skill
    capacitor-mobile/SKILL.md     ← Mobile/Capacitor skill
    vitest-playwright/SKILL.md    ← Testing skill
    code-review-carenet/SKILL.md  ← Code review skill
    spec-writing-carenet/SKILL.md ← Planning/spec skill
  instructions/
    frontend.instructions.md      ← Auto-applies to src/frontend/**
    backend-services.instructions.md ← Auto-applies to src/backend/**
    migrations.instructions.md    ← Auto-applies to supabase/migrations/**
    tests.instructions.md         ← Auto-applies to e2e/**, *.test.ts
    locales.instructions.md       ← Auto-applies to src/locales/**
```

## GitHub Copilot Folder Structure (Reference)

As of 2025-2026, GitHub Copilot reads from these `.github/` subfolders in VS Code:

| Folder | Purpose | How Copilot uses it |
|---|---|---|
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Always-on project instructions | Injected into every Copilot Chat session automatically |
| `.github/agents/*.agent.md` | Custom agent profiles | Available in the agent picker (`@agent-name`) in Copilot Chat |
| `.github/prompts/*.prompt.md` | Reusable prompt starters | Available as slash commands (`/prompt-name`) in chat |
| `.github/instructions/*.instructions.md` | Scoped file-level rules | Auto-applied when Copilot edits files matching the `applyTo:` glob |
| `.github/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` | Agent Skills (workflow packages) | Auto-loaded by Copilot when relevant; also invokable with `/skill-name` |

See `.ai-setup/README.md` for the full cross-IDE map.

## Simple Workflow

1. Update rules/skills in the pack at `Rules&Skills/packs/carenet-2`.
2. Run `npm run ai:sync` in `CareNet 2` to regenerate Cursor/Windsurf/agents files.
3. If you also changed what agents, prompts, or skills Copilot should have, edit `.github/` directly.
4. Start a new IDE/agent session if needed so the updated rules are picked up.
